refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a real-time communication protocol optimized for low latency and reliability
Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Segment the teleoperation system into modular components separating command transmission, processing, and feedback
Use adaptive data compression and transmission rate control to maintain latency within limits
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules
coupling_check.notes: By isolating the communication channel and prioritizing teleoperation data, the design parameter supports independence from other system functions, reducing coupling and helping meet latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Prioritization mechanisms do not starve other critical system communications
Local processing delays are minimal compared to communication latency
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including local predictive control as a complementary DP to mitigate latency spikes and improve safety under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing remote control commands and feedback communication

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for sending and receiving prioritized teleoperation data streams

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface for command and control task messages related to teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion across unrelated subsystems.
Vehicle module includes hardware and local control; DP would diffuse heavily outside this module.
Highly cohesive for DP; communication channel for teleoperation commands and feedback fits naturally here with minimal diffusion.
Safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; DP does not fit well here and would cause high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the dedicated communication channel for prioritized teleoperation commands and feedback, minimizing diffusion and aligning with its responsibility for remote control communication.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on underlying network infrastructure managed outside Teleoperation may cause some coupling.
Changes in communication protocols might require coordination with NGHE Solution or Vehicle modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow, which is essential to achieve the required end-to-end latency for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on underlying network infrastructure managed outside Teleoperation may cause some coupling.
Changes in communication protocols might require coordination with NGHE Solution or Vehicle modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating it is a highly cohesive boundary for the DP. This minimizes diffusion risk and aligns well with the responsibility for managing teleoperation communication.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Teleoperation module's interfaces (Telemetry and Tasking).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation module is clear and well justified, with explicit interfaces supporting prioritized teleoperation data streams.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document and manage dependencies on underlying network infrastructure to mitigate coupling risks.
Establish coordination protocols with NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to handle communication protocol changes and maintain interface stability.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP directly addresses the FR with a dedicated communication channel ensuring required latency, and the IM (Teleoperation module) provides a cohesive and well-contained implementation with clear interfaces. Although there are some diffusion risks related to dependencies on network infrastructure and coordination with other modules, these are manageable through documented dependencies and coordination protocols. Given the high safety criticality, the current level is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further decomposition at this time.

revise.why: Explicitly document and manage dependencies on underlying network infrastructure and establish coordination protocols with NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to mitigate coupling risks and maintain interface stability.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module can reliably measure and enforce end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions.
Dependencies on network infrastructure and other modules can be effectively managed through documentation and coordination protocols.
open_risks: Potential coupling due to dependency on external network infrastructure.
Need for coordination with NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules to handle communication protocol changes.
evidence_needed: Verification results showing end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under test conditions.
Documentation of dependency management and coordination protocols with related modules.
